User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user (employee) of the VENDIXPRO system, distinct from authentication credentials which are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: Role of the user within the system (e.g., 'admin', 'seller').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines categories for products to help with organization and reporting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Information about product suppliers for inventory management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

contactPerson.description: Name of the primary contact person at the supplier.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Details for products managed in the inventory and sold at the POS.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique code for the product.

costPrice.description: Cost price of the product (for profit calculation).

stockQuantity.description: Current quantity of the product in stock.

minStockQuantity.description: Minimum stock quantity to trigger a low stock alert.

isActive.description: Indicates if the product is active and available for sale.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category for this product. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier of this product. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product details were last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer, particularly for managing credit accounts ('cuentas corrientes').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

currentBalance.description: Current credit/debt balance for the customer. Positive for credit, negative for debt.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

Sale.title: Sale
Sale.description: Represents a completed sales transaction from the Point of Sale.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sale entity.

totalAmount.description: Sum of all sale items before taxes and discounts.

taxAmount.description: Total tax applied to the sale.

discountAmount.description: Total discount applied to the sale.

finalAmount.description: Final amount paid by the customer after taxes and discounts.

userId.description: Reference to the User (seller) who processed the sale. (Relationship: User 1:N Sale)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer if the sale was made to a registered customer. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Sale)

cashRegisterSessionId.description: Reference to the CashRegisterSession in which this sale occurred. (Relationship: CashRegisterSession 1:N Sale)

SaleItem.title: SaleItem
SaleItem.description: Represents an individual product item within a sales transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SaleItem entity.

saleId.description: Reference to the Sale this item belongs to. (Relationship: Sale 1:N SaleItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product sold. (Relationship: Product 1:N SaleItem)

productName.description: Name of the product at the time of sale (denormalized for historical accuracy).

unitPrice.description: Selling price of one unit of the product at the time of sale.

discountPerItem.description: Discount applied to this specific item.

subtotal.description: Subtotal for this item (quantity * unitPrice - discountPerItem).

AccountTransaction.title: AccountTransaction
AccountTransaction.description: Records financial transactions related to customer credit accounts (payments, charges).

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccountTransaction entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer whose account is affected. (Relationship: Customer 1:N AccountTransaction)

amount.description: Amount of the transaction. Positive for payments, negative for new charges/debt.

saleId.description: Reference to the Sale if this transaction is related to a specific sale (e.g., a credit sale). (Relationship: Sale 1:N AccountTransaction)

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N AccountTransaction)

CashRegisterSession.title: CashRegisterSession
CashRegisterSession.description: Manages the daily opening and closing of the cash register, tracking its balance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CashRegisterSession entity.

openingDateTime.description: Timestamp when the cash register session was opened.

closingDateTime.description: Timestamp when the cash register session was closed.

initialBalance.description: Amount of cash in the register at the start of the session.

finalBalance.description: Amount of cash in the register at the end of the session.

userId.description: Reference to the User who opened/closed the session. (Relationship: User 1:N CashRegisterSession)

CashRegisterMovement.title: CashRegisterMovement
CashRegisterMovement.description: Records individual cash inflows, outflows, and adjustments within a cash register session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CashRegisterMovement entity.

cashRegisterSessionId.description: Reference to the CashRegisterSession this movement belongs to. (Relationship: CashRegisterSession 1:N CashRegisterMovement)

movementDateTime.description: Timestamp when the cash movement occurred.

type.description: Type of movement (e.g., 'Sale Income', 'Expense Debit', 'Adjustment Credit', 'Adjustment Debit').

amount.description: Amount of cash involved in the movement.

description.description: Description or reason for the cash movement.

sourceEntityId.description: Optional reference to the ID of the related entity (e.g., Sale ID, Expense ID).

sourceEntityType.description: Optional type of the related entity (e.g., 'Sale', 'Expense') for traceability.

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded this movement. (Relationship: User 1:N CashRegisterMovement)

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExpenseCategory entity.

name.description: Name of the expense category (e.g., 'Rent', 'Utilities', 'Office Supplies').

description.description: Brief description of the expense category.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Records operational expenses of the business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

expenseDateTime.description: Timestamp when the expense occurred or was recorded.

expenseCategoryId.description: Reference to the category of this expense. (Relationship: ExpenseCategory 1:N Expense)

cashRegisterMovementId.description: Optional reference to the CashRegisterMovement that records the cash outflow for this expense. (Relationship: CashRegisterMovement 1:N Expense)

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded this expense. (Relationship: User 1:N Expense)

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, primarily for system users/employees. Access is path-based for the user's own profile (userId == request.auth.uid). Contains the user's role field for display logic.

Dedicated collection for admin role-based authorization. The existence of a document with `adminId` as its ID grants admin permissions, enabling efficient 'existence-based' checks in security rules without `get()` calls. The document content is typically a simple marker.

Dedicated collection for manager role-based authorization. The existence of a document with `managerId` as its ID grants manager permissions, enabling efficient 'existence-based' checks in security rules without `get()` calls. The document content is typically a simple marker.

Dedicated collection for seller role-based authorization. The existence of a document with `sellerId` as its ID grants seller permissions, enabling efficient 'existence-based' checks in security rules without `get()` calls. The document content is typically a simple marker.
Global collection for product categories. Managed by users with 'admin' or 'manager' roles.
Global collection for supplier details. Managed by users with 'admin' or 'manager' roles.
Global collection for product inventory. Managed by users with 'admin' or 'manager' roles, readable by 'seller' roles.
Global collection for customer profiles, especially for managing credit accounts. Managed by users with 'admin' or 'manager' roles, with sellers able to create/update as part of sales/transactions.

Records individual sales transactions. Each sale includes the `userId` of the seller who processed it. Access to sales is granted to the `userId` owner or users with 'manager'/'admin' roles. Supports QAPs by enforcing `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` for sellers listing their own sales.

Subcollection storing individual product items within a sale. Includes denormalized `saleOwnerId` (copied from `Sale.userId`) for authorization independence, allowing secure access rules without requiring a `get()` call to the parent `Sale` document.

Records financial transactions related to customer credit accounts. Each transaction includes the `userId` of the recorder and the `customerId`. Access is granted to the `userId` owner or users with 'manager'/'admin' roles.

Manages daily opening and closing of the cash register. Each session is associated with a `userId` (the employee who opened/closed it). Access to sessions is granted to the `userId` owner or users with 'manager'/'admin' roles. Supports QAPs.

Subcollection recording individual cash inflows, outflows, and adjustments within a specific cash register session. Includes denormalized `sessionOwnerId` (copied from `CashRegisterSession.userId`) for authorization independence, allowing secure access rules without requiring a `get()` call to the parent `CashRegisterSession` document.
Global collection defining categories for operational expenses. Managed by users with 'admin' or 'manager' roles.

Records individual operational expenses. Each expense is associated with a `userId` (the employee who recorded it). Access to expenses is granted to the `userId` owner or users with 'manager'/'admin' roles. Supports QAPs.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for VENDIXPRO prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the mandated design principles, especially Authorization Independence and secure Query As a Policy (QAP) enforcement. The overall strategy combines flat, global collections for core business entities with hierarchical structures for user-initiated transactional data, incorporating denormalization for robust authorization.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC):** To eliminate expensive and problematic `get()` calls for general role-based authorization, we implement DBAC using 'Existence over Content' collections. Instead of querying a user's role from `/users/{userId}`, we introduce dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{userId}`, `/roles_manager/{userId}`, and `/roles_seller/{userId}`. A user's role is determined by the *existence* of their UID as a document ID in one of these collections. This allows security rules to check `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` which is efficient and does not create hierarchical dependencies that break atomic operations.
2.  **Subcollection Denormalization:** For subcollections whose authorization relies on parent document attributes, the critical authorization fields are denormalized. Specifically:
    *   `SaleItem` documents, residing in `/sales/{saleId}/sale_items/{saleItemId}`, will include a denormalized `saleOwnerId` field, copied from the parent `Sale.userId`. This ensures that security rules for `SaleItem` do not need to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `Sale` document to determine ownership or permissions.
    *   `CashRegisterMovement` documents, residing in `/cash_register_sessions/{cashRegisterSessionId}/cash_register_movements/{cashRegisterMovementId}`, will include a denormalized `sessionOwnerId` field, copied from the parent `CashRegisterSession.userId`. This enables independent authorization checks for movements without querying the session details.

**Query As a Policy (QAPs):**
1.  **Global Collections:** For entities like `/categories`, `/suppliers`, `/products`, `/customers`, and `/expense_categories`, access is granted based on the user's role. For instance, any user with a 'seller' role (checked via `exists(/roles_seller/$(request.auth.uid))`) might have read access to all products. This fulfills QAPs as the entire collection (or a significant, logically grouped portion) is accessible based on the user's role, thus 'rules are not filters' for these broad permissions.
2.  **User-Scoped Data with Role Overrides:** For transactional data such as `/sales`, `/expenses`, and `/cash_register_sessions`, each document includes a `userId` field. To enforce QAPs:
    *   **User-Specific Lists:** A regular seller (`roles_seller` only) attempting to `list` documents in these collections *must* include a `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` filter in their query. Security rules will enforce this, ensuring that users only retrieve their own data unless explicitly authorized otherwise.
    *   **Manager/Admin Overrides:** Users with 'manager' or 'admin' roles (checked via `exists(/roles_manager/$(request.auth.uid))` or `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) are allowed to `list` all documents within these collections without specific `userId` filters. This provides a secure and scalable way to grant broader oversight capabilities without compromising the QAP principle for individual users.

**Structural Segregation & Predictability:**
All documents within a collection share a homogeneous security posture. Data types are consistent within collections, and naming conventions (e.g., plural collection names, descriptive wildcards like `{productId}`, explicit `status` fields) enhance clarity and debuggability.
